stimulus: In 1817, mathematician Sophie Germain submitted a memoir on elastic surfaces to the Paris Academy of Sciences. Germain claimed that her analysis extended an unpublished argument by the late Leonhard Euler. Several reviewers, including physicist Siméon Poisson, were ______blank later mathematicians have confirmed that Euler did indeed leave behind notes on the very surfaces Germain treated.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence contains two independent clauses, each with a subject and a verb: “Several reviewers...were doubtful” and “later mathematicians have confirmed....” These clauses can be grammatically joined by a comma and the coordinating conjunction “but.”

Choice A is incorrect because it results in a run-on sentence. Choice C is incorrect because two independent clauses can’t be joined by the coordinating conjunction “but” alone—a comma is also required. Choice D is incorrect because it results in a comma splice.
